title: Replace emoji icons with CSS/SVG inline icons and refine design tokens
description: Replace all emoji characters used as icons throughout panel/index.js with inline SVG or CSS-based icon alternatives. Audit and refine the existing CSS custom properties design token system for spacing, color, and typography consistency following ui-ux-pro-max best practices. This covers the CSS style section (~500 lines) and all emoji references in both HTML template and JS methods.

criteria: Zero emoji characters remain in panel/index.js icon positions (gear, star, fire, arrow, plus, refresh, etc.)
All icons replaced with inline SVG elements or CSS-generated icons that render correctly in Electron 2-4
CSS custom properties audit complete: spacing scale consistent (4/8/12/16/24px), color tokens follow 4.5:1 contrast ratio for text on backgrounds
Typography tokens refined: body text minimum 13px, line-height 1.5 for body text, monospace font for code elements
No visual regression in dark theme appearance - indigo accent (#6366f1) and slate palette preserved
convergence.verification: Open panel in Cocos Creator 2.x, visually verify all icons render as SVG/CSS, check design token consistency across all UI states (default, hover, active, focused)

files.target: style property (CSS custom properties and icon-related rules)
files.change: Refine CSS design tokens for spacing/color/typography consistency; add SVG icon CSS classes or inline SVG styles; remove emoji-dependent styling

files.target: template property (HTML) and JS methods generating innerHTML
files.change: Replace all emoji characters (gear, star, fire, arrow, plus, refresh, etc.) with inline SVG elements or CSS-based icon spans

implementation: Catalog all emoji icon usages in panel/index.js: search for emoji characters in style, template, and JS string literals (innerHTML assignments)

Design inline SVG replacements for each icon: gear (settings), star (favorite), fire (frequent), arrows (navigation), plus (add), refresh (reload), search magnifier, close X, etc. - all must be simple path-based SVGs that work in Electron 2-4
Create CSS classes for icon sizing and coloring using currentColor for theme compatibility: .icon-sm (16px), .icon-md (20px), .icon-lg (24px)
Replace each emoji in template HTML string with corresponding <svg> element wrapped in icon class
Replace each emoji in JS innerHTML generation methods (addCom, refreshCurComs, onInput2 result rendering, settings page) with SVG markup
Audit CSS custom properties: verify spacing scale (xs:4, sm:8, md:12, lg:16, xl:20, 2xl:24), ensure color contrast meets 4.5:1 ratio, add missing tokens if needed
Refine typography tokens: ensure readable-font-size (min 13px for editor context), consistent line-height (1.5 body, 1.3 headings), proper font-weight scale
Test in Cocos Creator 2.x that all SVG icons render correctly at various panel sizes

reference.pattern: CSS custom properties design token system on :host
reference.examples: Current design tokens at panel/index.js:14-66. Follow ui-ux-pro-max no-emoji-icons rule (SKILL.md line 79). Use inline SVG with currentColor for theme-aware icons.

rationale.chosen_approach: Inline SVG icons with CSS classes for sizing, replacing emoji characters one-by-one while refining the design token system in the same pass
alternatives_considered: CSS-only icons using pseudo-elements and borders (limited icon vocabulary)
Base64-encoded icon font (adds complexity, harder to maintain)
Unicode symbols instead of emoji (still inconsistent across platforms)
rationale.tradeoffs: Inline SVGs increase the HTML template string size, but ensure consistent rendering and accessibility

manual_checks: All icons render correctly in Cocos Creator 2.x panel at default size (450x120)
Icons scale properly when panel is resized (docked vs floating)
Dark theme colors and contrast ratios verified visually
No emoji fallback glyphs visible anywhere in the UI
success_metrics: All text-on-background combinations meet 4.5:1 contrast ratio
Spacing scale uses consistent 4px base unit
